annotations.label: Chern classes as topological invariants

anchor.snippet: They are topological invariants associated with vector bundles on a smooth manifold

annotations.note: Vector bundles are formalized (Mathlib.Topology.VectorBundle.Basic) but Chern classes as their topological invariants do not exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chern classes distinguish bundles

anchor.snippet: if the Chern classes of a pair of vector bundles do not agree, then the vector bundles are different

annotations.note: No Chern class invariant exists in Mathlib, so this distinguishing property is absent.

annotations.label: Chern classes as curvature polynomials

anchor.snippet: the Chern classes can be expressed as polynomials in the coefficients of the curvature form

annotations.note: Neither Chern classes nor curvature forms of connections on bundles are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chern classes via pullback of universal bundle

anchor.snippet: the Chern classes of V can therefore be defined as the pullback of the Chern classes of the universal bundle

annotations.note: Classifying spaces, universal bundles, and Chern classes are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homotopy of classifying maps

anchor.snippet: the maps must be homotopic

annotations.note: Homotopy exists in Mathlib but the statement about classifying maps to a universal bundle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem motivation

anchor.snippet: the theorem saying one can't comb a hairy ball flat

annotations.note: The hairy ball / Poincaré–Hopf theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First Chern class of a line bundle

anchor.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchor.snippet: the only nontrivial Chern class is the first Chern class, which is an element of the second cohomology group of X

annotations.note: The first Chern class of a complex line bundle does not exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First Chern class equals Euler class (line bundle)

anchor.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchor.snippet: As it is the top Chern class, it equals the Euler class of the bundle

annotations.note: Neither Chern classes nor the Euler class of a bundle are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of line bundles by first Chern class

anchor.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchor.snippet: The first Chern class turns out to be a complete invariant with which to classify complex line bundles

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the algebraic Picard group classifying invertible modules/line bundles, but not the topological classification via the first Chern class in H².

anchors.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchors.snippet: The first Chern class turns out to be a complete invariant with which to classify complex line bundles

annotations.label: Tensor product corresponds to addition

anchor.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchor.snippet: the tensor product of complex line bundles corresponds to the addition in the second cohomology group

annotations.note: The Picard group's group law is tensor product (its CommGroup instance), but the isomorphism to additive H²(X;ℤ) via the first Chern class is not formalized.

anchors.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchors.snippet: the tensor product of complex line bundles corresponds to the addition in the second cohomology group

annotations.label: Chern classes not complete in higher rank

anchor.section: The Chern class of line bundles
anchor.snippet: For complex vector bundles of dimension greater than one, the Chern classes are not a complete invariant

annotations.note: Chern classes are absent from Mathlib, so this incompleteness statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chern form via characteristic polynomial

anchor.snippet: given as the coefficients of the characteristic polynomial of the curvature form

annotations.note: Chern–Weil theory and Chern forms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curvature form

anchor.snippet: with ω the connection form and d the exterior derivative

annotations.note: The curvature form of a connection on a vector bundle is not defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: with ω the connection form and d the exterior derivative

annotations.label: Chern classes independent of connection

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that the cohomology classes of the Chern forms do not depend on the choice of connection

annotations.note: This Chern–Weil invariance result is not formalized since Chern forms are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Top Chern class as Euler class

anchor.snippet: one simply defines the top Chern class of the bundle to be its Euler class

annotations.note: The Euler class of a bundle is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lower Chern classes via base change

anchor.snippet: The idea is to do base change to get a bundle of one-less rank

annotations.note: This inductive construction of Chern classes via base change is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex hairy ball theorem

anchor.section: The complex tangent bundle of the Riemann sphere
anchor.snippet: We prove the complex version of the hairy ball theorem : V has no section which is everywhere nonzero

annotations.note: This nonvanishing-section result for the Riemann sphere's tangent bundle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First Chern class of trivial bundle is zero

anchor.section: The complex tangent bundle of the Riemann sphere
anchor.snippet: the first Chern class of a trivial bundle is zero

annotations.note: Chern classes are absent from Mathlib, so this vanishing for trivial bundles is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: is not (ordinary) addition, but rather a formal group law

annotations.label: First Chern class on Chow groups

anchor.snippet: the first Chern class of a line bundle over a variety V is a homomorphism from CH( V ) to CH( V ) reducing degrees by 1

annotations.note: Chow groups and the first Chern class operator on them are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chern classes of an almost complex manifold

anchor.snippet: The Chern classes of M are thus defined to be the Chern classes of its tangent bundle

annotations.note: Almost complex structures and Chern classes of manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cobordism and Chern numbers

anchor.snippet: it is cobordant to M if and only if the Chern numbers of M ′ coincide with those of M

annotations.note: Cobordism theory and Chern numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: symplectic manifolds have a well-defined Chern class
